title: Blade Server ECC Memory Error Rate (Cisco UCS)
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" earliest=-24h
| search dn="sys/chassis-*/blade-*" AND (like(lower(cause),"%ecc%") OR like(lower(cause),"%memory%") OR like(lower(cause),"%dimm%"))
| stats count as fault_events by dn, code, severity
| sort -fault_events
description: Correctable ECC errors often precede uncorrectable failures and guest crashes. Trending per-blade memory error rates lets you schedule DIMM replacement during maintenance windows instead of reacting to sudden hardware loss.
value: Correctable ECC errors often precede uncorrectable failures and guest crashes. Trending per-blade memory error rates lets you schedule DIMM replacement during maintenance windows instead of reacting to sudden hardware loss.
implementation: (1) Ensure UCS Manager faults or CIMC memory events forward to Splunk with consistent `dn` naming; (2) baseline normal correctable rates per chassis; (3) alert when per-blade counts exceed baseline or severity is major/critical.
visualization: Bar chart (faults by blade), Table (top DIMM-related codes), Timechart (ECC-related fault rate).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, UCS Manager faults and SEL.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults"` with fields `dn`, `cause`, `code`, `severity`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Ensure UCS Manager faults or CIMC memory events forward to Splunk with consistent `dn` naming; (2) baseline normal correctable rates per chassis; (3) alert when per-blade counts exceed baseline or severity is major/critical.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" earliest=-24h
| search dn="sys/chassis-*/blade-*" AND (like(lower(cause),"%ecc%") OR like(lower(cause),"%memory%") OR like(lower(cause),"%dimm%"))
| stats count as fault_events by dn, code, severity
| sort -fault_events
```

Understanding this SPL

**Blade Server ECC Memory Error Rate (Cisco UCS)** ‚Äî Correctable ECC errors often precede uncorrectable failures and guest crashes. Trending per-blade memory error rates lets you schedule DIMM replacement during maintenance windows instead of reacting to sudden hardware loss.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_ucs` `sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults"` with fields `dn`, `cause`, `code`, `severity`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`, UCS Manager faults and SEL. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:faults. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dn, code, severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with Cisco UCS Manager (faults, equipment, and service profile context) for the same DNs and time range.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (faults by blade), Table (top DIMM-related codes), Timechart (ECC-related fault rate).
grandmaExplanation: Correctable ECC errors often precede uncorrectable failures and guest crashes ‚Äî so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
hardware: Cisco UCS B200 M6/M7, UCS X210c M6/M7, UCS X410c M6
knownFalsePositives: Fault clears during RMA, firmware, and intentional suppression in governed windows.
